goal: Generate v1 demo-scale Orbit relationship mockdata under repos/mockdata using a MiniMax Claude SDK-backed AI agent and deterministic validators.

success_criteria.description: The MiniMax-backed generation run creates the documented mockdata directory structure under repos/mockdata: dictionaries, seed, tests, scenarios, generated, exports, validators, and generation reports.

The v1 demo dataset reaches the design-document scale: 120-150 users, 10-12 events, 80-100 companies or organizations, 400-600 event participant records, 800-1200 relationship/connection records, at least 1000 interaction records, 300-500 recommendation records, and 100-200 golden matches.

The data respects the required Japanese business / Chinese business-community ratios, language distribution, persona templates, ten demo scenarios, negative cases, dirty data cases, and conservative low-confidence AI behavior from the design document.

Every generated recommendation, golden match, negative case, AI analysis, interaction, event participant, contact, and connection uses stable ids and resolvable references; the validator rejects dangling references, duplicate ids, score values outside 0-1, and empty generic goals such as 'meet interesting people' unless the record is explicitly a dirty-data case.

Generation reports prove the run used MiniMax Claude SDK environment variables without logging secrets, records prompts and validation summaries under harness-state/runs, and exports a local_seed.json plus demo_seed.json that can be consumed by a future Orbits database importer.

out_of_scope: Manually hand-designing individual mock records outside the AI agent workflow
Writing generated data into repos/orbits/shared/mock/fixtures.ts in this sprint
Adding live database writes, external CRM sync, email/calendar sends, embeddings, or vector indexes
Logging real MiniMax API keys, Anthropic tokens, OAuth tokens, cookies, or credentials
